meta.topic: My first company raised ₹10 Cr. Here's what I'd do differently on day 1.
meta.audience: First-time founders and early-stage Indian entrepreneurs

slides.headline: My first company raised ₹10 Cr at a ₹20 Cr valuation.
slides.subhead: It still didn't make it. Here's what I'd fix on day 1.
Not funding. Not product. Three gaps we didn't close early enough — legal infrastructure, brand credibility, and vendor fragmentation. Envesty is the answer to all three.

left.label: What we did
left.body: Used a template MOA. Skipped the co-founder agreement. No IP assignment for the domain or codebase. Found the gaps 18 months in.

right.label: What I'd do now
right.body: Day 1: shareholders agreement with vesting schedule + IP assignment to the company. Before the first hire. Before the first commit.

left.label: What we did
left.body: Launched with inflated claims and stock-photo aesthetics. Felt 'professional.' Cost us trust with the investors and clients who actually checked.

right.label: What I'd do now
right.body: Real founders, real photos, honest stage framing. 'Since Feb 2025' beats '5+ years of expertise' — because serious people know how to verify.

slides.headline: Gap 3 — Vendor fragmentation. Legal CA, GST CA, web agency, marketing agency — four vendors, four contracts, nobody owns the outcome. Every handoff is a delay and a blame gap. That's the exact problem Envesty is built to eliminate.
DM me.
slides.body: I'll share the day-1 setup checklist I built after Howdy — what we now run every new Envesty client through before anything else.

caption:
My first company raised ₹10 Cr at a ₹20 Cr valuation.

It still didn't make it to profitability.

I don't say that for sympathy. I say it because the three gaps that hurt us are the same ones I see in almost every founder we talk to at Envesty.

Gap 1 — Legal infrastructure
We used a template MOA. Skipped the co-founder agreement. No IP assignment for the domain or codebase. Found the gaps 18 months in, when it was expensive to fix.

Gap 2 — Brand credibility
We launched with inflated claims. Felt professional. Cost us trust with the investors and clients who actually looked us up.

Gap 3 — Vendor fragmentation
4 vendors, 4 contracts, nobody owning the outcome. Every handoff was a delay. Every delay had someone else to blame.

Envesty is the answer I wish we'd had in 2016.

DM me — I'll share the day-1 setup checklist I built after that experience. We now run every new client through it.

— Sushant

#FounderStory #StartupIndia #IndianFounders #Entrepreneurship #Envesty #LessonsLearned
hooks: The ₹10 Cr raise didn't save us. Here's what would have.
I'm building Envesty to fix the three gaps that ended my first company.
cta: DM me — I'll send the day-1 checklist I built after Howdy. We run every new Envesty client through it.
